datum_strategy.scheme: fixture-oriented motor mount datum using the base face and front hole row
notes: Use the base plate and four-hole pattern as drawing QA references only.

tolerance_policy.general: Carry source drawing tolerance note forward; do not infer additional tolerances.
notes: ISO 2768-m is carried from the source drawing metadata as a general note.

critical_features.description: Base plate envelope communicates the 120 mm by 80 mm by 10 mm machined support footprint.
critical_features.reason: Defines stock size, mounting face, and base plate intent.

critical_features.description: Vertical support web provides the motor mounting face and stiffness reference.
critical_features.reason: Communicates the raised support feature from the single-part source config.

critical_features.description: Support rib reinforces the web and should remain visible in front and iso views.
critical_features.reason: Shows the normalized central stiffness feature without relying on the deferred assembly config.

critical_features.description: Four mounting holes define the motor/base mounting interface.
critical_features.reason: Hole diameter and pattern location control the fastening interface.

required_dimensions.label: Support web height
required_dimensions.reason: Carries forward the source support web height.

required_dimensions.label: Support rib height
required_dimensions.reason: Carries forward the source support rib height.

required_dimensions.label: Four mounting hole diameters
required_dimensions.reason: Confirms the shared fastener clearance hole size.

required_dimensions.label: Mounting hole column spacing
required_dimensions.reason: Captures the horizontal spacing from x=20 mm to x=100 mm.

required_dimensions.label: Mounting hole row spacing
required_dimensions.reason: Captures the vertical spacing from y=15 mm to y=65 mm.

required_notes.text: 1 mm chamfer applied as a deburr edge break on the final machined bracket body.
